{"text":"Now I have all the info I need. Let me design the virtual library app with an archival aesthetic. Here's my plan:

**Aesthetic:**
- Archival stance: warm cream background, deep forest green primary, gold/amber accent
- Fonts: Playfair Display (serif display) + Libre Franklin (clean sans body) + DM Mono (labels/data)
- Color palette:
  - Background: #F5F0E8 (warm cream/parchment)
  - Foreground: #1A1612 (deep warm black)
  - Card: #EDE8DC (slightly deeper cream)
  - Primary: #2C4A1E (deep forest green)
  - Primary foreground: #F5F0E8
  - Secondary: #E8E0CF
  - Accent: #8B6914 (warm gold/amber)
  - Muted: #D8D0BC
  - Border: rgba(26, 22, 18, 0.15)

**Features:**
1. Login / Register modal/screen
2. Main library dashboard with:
   - Book catalog with rental options
   - My Books section (rented books with return option)
   - Suggestion box
   - Profile info

**State:**

Now I'm mapping out the state management I'll need: authentication status, which view the user is on, modal visibility, and the book data itself. I'll structure the app with an auth screen that handles login and registration, then once logged in, show a main dashboard with a header navigation for browsing the catalog, viewing rented books, submitting suggestions, and accessing profile settings. I'm planning a diverse book collection across multiple genres with realistic metadata like titles, authors, publication years, and cover images.

For the book covers, I'm going to use gradient-colored cards instead of trying to source specific images—this keeps the implementation clean and gives the app a polished, cohesive look. I'll set up the core state structure to track authentication, current view, modal state, user info, and the list of rented books.

Writing the component structure... Writing the React component... Continuing with more book entries... Continuing with the book catalog... Adding more book entries... Setting up state variables for the user, view navigation, rented books, and mobile menu, along with form fields for authentication and book suggestions.

Now I'm adding state for search and filtering by genre, plus a toast notification system that automatically dismisses after a few seconds, and then implementing the login handler. Writing authentication handlers... Now I'm setting up the genre filter options and creating a filtered list of books based on both search query and selected genre. Then I'm mapping the rented books to include their full book details, and finally checking if the user is authenticated before rendering either the auth screen or the main app.

I'm going to write out the complete component implementation with an archival aesthetic—cream parchment backgrounds, forest green accents, and gold highlights. The design uses Playfair Display for headings with Libre Franklin for body text, book cards styled like spines, and a formal suggestion form. The auth screen will have a dramatic full-page layout with typography on the left and the login form on the right, while the main app uses a sidebar on desktop and a hamburger menu on mobile.

Writing the component structure... Writing the complete App component... Populating the book collection... Adding more book entries... Continuing with the book catalog... Writing component code... Writing component markup... Writing component structure... Writing component markup... Writing component markup... Writing form fields... Writing component styles...

Now I'm setting up the sidebar navigation with different view options like the catalog, my books, and suggestions, each with their own icon and label. Building the sidebar navigation... Writing component markup... Writing the catalog view component... Writing component markup... Writing the book grid layout... Writing component markup... Writing the MyBooksView component... Building the rental card layout... Writing component markup... Writing component markup... Writing form markup... Building form inputs... Finishing up the form with a submit button that has the send icon and Portuguese text, then closing out the component structure. Now I'm thinking through the full App component that handles authentication state and renders either the login screen or the main application, and I'm considering adding a shipping request feature so users can have books physically delivered to them—maybe as a separate button or form section in the My Books view.

Let me clarify the workflow: users should be able to rent books from the catalog, request delivery to their address, and then return them when done. I'll add "Solicitar Envio" and "Devolver" buttons next to each rented book, with the delivery request expanding inline to show a simple address form rather than opening a modal, keeping the interface clean and straightforward.

Now I'm ready to write out the complete implementation with all the necessary imports, types, and components in one cohesive pass, including the mobile sidebar overlay that appears when the hamburger menu is clicked.

Writing the full implementation... Writing the complete implementation...","providerOptions":{}}
